transcript: Hey, this is Max Braxton. What’s goin’ on?
Uh, I don’t—I don’t know you, Max Braxton.
Yes, Yes, I just moved in; I’m your new neighbor. I got your number from my friend at the DMV. Good friend of mine up ‘ere! And since we live in the same neighborhood—How’s it goin’, man?
Hey, Travis! Man, come on, just help me out for a sec., okay? It’s Max.
I don’t—I don’t know you, dude.
I’m your new neighbor, man! I just live on the same street. I just have a problem with my garage door. I just need you to help me real quick. I can give you four or five dollars.
One, I’m at work right now, so that’s a no. But I still don’t—
—Dude, what street do you live on?
Yeah, but you said that you live on my street. So what street do you live on?

I do! I just moved to Lexington from Colorado, like, this week. I can bring you the lever, you push it, and I’ll just tell you when my garage door’s moving, okay? That’s all’s I need! My friend said you’d help me out.
At the DMV. He looked up your number for me. [sfx]—
—Who at the DMV? Because it’s not [stammers] it’s kinda against the law to be giving out phone numbers at the DMV; they’re not the police department. So I’d like to know who gave you my number.
Well, be cool, man! I just need help with my garage door. It’ll take, like, 20, 30 minutes. I can give ya five, six dollars!—
—Well, I’m at work—Well five or six dollars, dude, ain’t worth my time. That’s a pack of cigarettes!
Well, per hour, I mean. Just be cool!—
—I’ll s—I’ll come over to your house about five, okay? And we’ll just hook up the—
—No, do not show up at my house or you will get a gun in your fuckin’ face!
Just be cool, dude! This is Max Braxton, man!
Dude, I don’t fuckin’ know a Max Braxton. Stop calling me!
Hey, I’ll give you ten dollars! I mean [delays]
This is Max, your new neighbor, man.
No! You’re not my fuckin’ neighbor! Quit fuckin’ calling me! Fuck you! I don’t wanna help you. Quit fuckin’ calling me!
I’m just gonna stop by, okay? I got the door to go up on my garage. I just need your help bringin’ it back down—
—I don’t give a fuck about your garage!
I can give ya ten dollars!
Pull it, motherfucker! Put your fuckin’ purse down and pull it, bitch!
Our friend at the DMV’s gonna be torqued if you don’t help me out.
I don’t have a fuckin’ friend in the DMV, dumbass! I know who works at the DMV. I don’t know any of ‘em!
"Sherman Jump" sounds like a male. I’m pretty sure everybody at my DMV is a female, so…
Oh, he’s a hundred percent male! And he’s gonna take ya down if you don’t give me a hand here, ya got it? So I’m gonna stop by in about 20 minutes and we’ll just work it out! Boom! We’re done!
How are we gonna work out your garage?
I’ll bring you the lever, you push it—
—I—I don’t wanna help you! Why don’t you call somebody else?
Dude, I just moved down here from Colorado, like I told you. [delays]
I have a cellphone and I have a landline. [tremolo guitar]
Why are you fuckin’ glitchin’ out like this is somethin’ else?
I am on my cellphone right now. You want a piece of me—
Hey, yeah—have to get a pickup. [exhales]
I’d like to get a—pick up some food today, please.
I want to get, ah, extra cheese, okay? And, ah, extra bean, okay?
No, I can’t understand what you’re sayin’. No, I can’t understand what—what are you s—what are you saying?
I want to pickup for lunch for pickup. [exhales]
Oh! [delay] I’m trying to get extra cheese and extra bean with my order.
Two different types of cheese, half-and-half, for the rice. Extra large. How much is it for it—for this much?
—what you’re saying, that you want “extra cheese, half-and-half”. What are you saying, bruh? You just come on in—
—place your order when you come in!
I just wanna pay now, you know? For pickup—
—‘Kay, you want a pint of rice?
No, I want extra corn, extra large—
—Corn, what corn? What corn? We don’t have corn—
You’re just playing games. You were saying, “beans, rice extra ch”—What do you want?—
—You’re gonna have to just come in, okay? I’m not dealing with this right now, okay? You just gotta come in—
—Please I have a credit card for you!
I don’t—I don’t understand what you’re saying!
How much is it, [laughs] you know? Come on—
Hey bud, you gotta stop calling because we’re—
—You’re not making sense! You’re not making sense! You’re wasting our time! If you start making sense, I can take your order.
This is Max KFC [Keyacee? K of C?]
Okay! What are you looking for, sir? What are you looking for?
We don’t have corn! We don’t have corn, sir! We don’t have corn! We have bean—
—You come to me with beef and corn, okay, to me. You pick me up! Okay?—
Yes. And I eat in the store.
O—okay, but we don’t have corn. I’m ju—I’m explaining that to you; you’re not understanding! We don’t have corn.
Okay, I pick it up. I pick it up.
You—I need four corn, four extra cheese, that’s it!
Four corn what? That’s the pro—
—This is Max, brother. I need to eat today. You make me run around and around, you know?
I don’t make you run around! Excuse me, I don’t make you run around! I don’t make you do nothin’, dude! You make me pick up the phone, waste ten minutes from my life!
You run around and around! What’s that about? You know, friend, I need corn and bean today, that’s it!
Okay, you come here—you come here and you order it, okay?
With the beef and bean, yeah.
Okay—Okay—I’m a—I’m a—I’m just gonna tape this and I’m gonna call the officer and he can deal with you, okay?
Oh, you’re gonna push me downtown?
I don’t make you run around, okay?—
—I don’t do that to you. I call for extra cheese, you know, one time!—
—We’re gonna put your phone on block! Have a good day! Don’t call back! It’s gonna be on block!—
—I’m gonna put you on the ground!
Okay. I’m gonna call the police and they can call you, okay? Thank you!
Hello, this is Tony. How can I help you?
Tony! Name’s Marty KFC. How are ya doin’ today, sir?
Good! What can I do for you?
Yeah, I’m right here in Mankato and I got a new house. I was lookin’ to spruce things up here.
Ah, no, sir! No, sir! I, ah, I saw an infomercial, is what I saw about a special kinda polymer flooring. It’s almost like a cork board type of floor board material. And I was hoping—
—We are fresh outta flooring options, but we do have flowers, ‘cause we’re a florist, not a floor-ist!
No, I got this number up at the grocery store. Guy’s a friend of mine up ‘ere. Put me in touch—
Do you have a showroom, or how many people are on your sales force, sir? [sfx]
[laughs] Aah! You’re funny! We’re—we’re—we’re a flower—
—No, sir. I got about thirty-two hundred square feet, here, I’m trying to cover.
[laughs] [stammers] We are a—a flower shop, not a floor shop! [laughs]
Buddy, I got nearly 3400 square feet, here: family of five!
—I—I would check your local, ah, tile store [chuckles]—
—Good friend of mine sent me up here. He said you could hook me up. I’d like a nice glossy cork on a finish to the floorboard. [sfx]
[chuckles] Aha—alright, man! I love it; it’s great. Ah, good job. But if you’re seriously looking for flooring [laughs] then you—
I was looking for, ah, some big hog.
Where do I have to go, my friend? [sfx]
Oh I really don—I really don’t know.
Do you have any Juice Gas?
I don’t know what that is.
You don’t know what what is?
What’d you ask me, did I have what? Whatever you asked me just now. I don’t know what it is—but I don’t know what you say.
Listen, listen: [unintelligible] you gonna find dick bullet in yo ass! Don’t call this number again.
The dick in yo mouth! Okay?
Hey, yeah. I was lookin’ for some “big hog”! [exhales]
Well, h—where can I get it, friend?
[in background] [stammers] Hang up the phone! Don’t answer the phone! That motherfucker! Don’t answer the phone!
Yes, can I get any spleen from you guys?
You know, it’s like a kidney or a liver, sorta [delay] [sfx] Do you have any of that at all?
I got a dinner party tonight. [sfx]
No, I need to get some hog [exhales] Can you hook me up or what? I’m at a payphone here, man! [sfx]
Can I get any Juice Gas off you? [sfx]
Somebody wants some skin, some wha—
—[employee interrupts in background] You got that folk? You got that folk?
Hello? The fuck is this shit?! [laugh]
Up In Smoke. This is Tom!
Oh, Hi! I wanted to get, like, a gravity bong. [sfx]

Uh. From what I know of wh—what you just mentioned, that’s something th—th—we would make back when I was younger ourselves out of a bucket and an empty milk bottle or somethin’—plastic jug— and you’d submerge the jug in the water. That’s what “I” know of a gravity bong. We have tons of water pipes, but nothing’s called a “gravity bong”.
Do you have any Juice Gas?
“Juice Gas”? I don’t even know what that is. I have never heard of that and I don’t know what it is. So no, we don’t have it.
Yeah, we definitely don’t have that.
You got any Mysterious Bliss up ‘ere? [exhales] [delay]
Hey, guy, yeah, I’m at a payphone here. Can you just help me out with the Juice Gas if I ride the bus up ‘ere?
We don’t have Juice Gas. I don’t know what that is; I’ve never heard of it.
Whadda you got on the Mysterious Bliss up ‘ere? [exhales] [delay]
No! I just got off the phone with you. We don’t have any of the things you’re asking for. I’ve never even heard of them. And it seems to me like you’re pulling my leg!
“Can you taste the sound of Mysterious Bliss?” Remember that commercial?—
